decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using low-cost creatures and burn spells. By efficiently utilizing resources and maintaining pressure on the opponent, the goal is to win the game as fast as possible before the opponent can stabilize their board. The deck is designed to be aggressive and proactive, aiming to overwhelm the opponent with a constant stream of threats and direct damage spells. With a strong emphasis on speed and efficiency, this deck aims to take control of the game early on and maintain that advantage until victory.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to quickly deploy threats onto the battlefield. The deck includes various Goblin tribal synergies, such as Goblin Ringleader and Goblin Piledriver, to further enhance the tribe's strength. Additionally, the deck has some removal options like Gempalm Incinerator and Mogg Fanatic to deal with opposing threats. The mana base includes utility lands like Rishadan Port and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain a strong board presence to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Elf tribe's abilities to generate mana quickly and efficiently, allowing for a fast and aggressive approach to overwhelm the opponent. By playing multiple mana-producing creatures like Llanowar Elves and Priest of Titania, and utilizing cards like Food Chain and Gaea's Cradle, the deck aims to ramp up its resources quickly to play powerful creatures like Brass Herald and Verdeloth the Ancient. With a mix of creature ramp and powerful finishers, this deck aims to swarm the board and close out the game before the opponent has a chance to recover.

This deck focuses on flooding the board with Elves to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creature-based strategies. Using cards like Priest of Titania and Quirion Ranger to generate massive amounts of mana, the deck aims to ramp up to powerful threats like Deranged Hermit and Kamahl, Fist of Krosa. Survival of the Fittest serves as a powerful tool for tutoring key creatures, while cards like Wellwisher provide lifegain to sustain the aggression. With Gaea's Cradle as a key mana source, this deck excels at quickly building a formidable board presence and applying relentless pressure.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Hypnotic Specter and Ravenous Rats to disrupt the opponent's hand, while also dealing damage with cards like Grim Lavamancer and Lavaborn Muse, the deck aims to pressure the opponent into making suboptimal plays. The inclusion of cards like Cursed Scroll and The Rack also adds additional direct damage and pressure to the opponent. Overall, the key strategy of the deck is to maintain an aggressive tempo while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to use cards like Attunement and Frantic Search to set up the combo of Replenish, Opalescence, Parallax Tide, and Parallax Wave. By constantly bringing back enchantments from the graveyard with Replenish, players can generate a powerful board presence with creatures that can continuously exile opponents' permanents. The deck also includes control elements like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing to handle any threats, while utilizing lands like Ancient Tomb and City of Traitors to ramp up mana production.

This deck focuses on using Stasis to lock down the opponent's resources and then utilizing various card draw, counterspells, and bounce spells to maintain control and eventually win the game through a combo finish. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and prevent them from effectively playing spells while finding the right pieces to assemble a winning combination. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Black Vise and Root Maze to further hinder the opponent's strategy. It is a versatile and strategic deck that requires careful planning and timing to execute effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using removal spells such as Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate, as well as hand disruption with Duress and Gerrard's Verdict. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Exalted Angel and Decree of Justice. The land base includes utility lands like Wasteland and Dust Bowl to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The goal is to outlast the opponent and win the game through card advantage and efficient removal spells.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing a combination of counterspells, removal, and board wipes to maintain board control. It also aims to stall the game with "Standstill" and limit the opponent's resources while gradually building up its own win conditions. The inclusion of cards like "Parallax Tide" and "Decree of Justice" provides additional control options, while cards like "Seal of Cleansing" offer flexibility against various threats. The deck's mana base is designed to support its control elements and ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing countermagic, removal spells, and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It aims to stall the game by utilizing lands such as Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave, while also using cards like Parallax Tide and Decree of Justice to create threats when necessary. The deck's goal is to establish a powerful control of the board while maintaining a steady stream of card advantage and answers to the opponent's threats.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through disruption and resource denial, utilizing cards like Cabal Therapy, Duress, and Contamination to strip away opponents' key cards and prevent them from building up their board. This deck also aims to generate card advantage and maintain board presence through cards like Zombie Infestation and Nether Spirit, while utilizing Mishra's Factory and Wasteland to pressure opponents and disrupt their mana base. Overall, this deck aims to outlast opponents through attrition and grind out victories through its strong control elements.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with enchantments like Parallax Wave and Parallax Tide, then casting Replenish to bring them all back into play. The combo of Opalescence and the Parallax cards can produce a powerful army of enchantment creatures, while cards like Chain of Vapor and Seal of Cleansing provide disruption and protection. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of enchantments and win the game through sheer advantage. The deck's mana base is designed to support the combo and maximize efficiency.

The main focus of this deck is to assemble the combo of Phyrexian Devourer and Fling, allowing you to quickly mill your entire library and deal a massive amount of damage to your opponent. Altar of Dementia serves as an alternate win condition in case the combo is disrupted. The deck also includes cards like Tinker and Oath of Druids to help find the combo pieces quickly, while also including tools like Defense Grid and Tangle Wire to protect the combo from disruption. The mana base is designed to support the combo and ensure consistency in executing your game plan.
